name: Polymyxin B
executiveSummary: Preoperative decolonization in short-bowel patients, combined with oral metronidazole.
indications:
## Main indications

Preoperative decolonization in short-bowel patients, combined with oral metronidazole.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: 1.5–2.5 mg/kg/day IV divided every 12 h (convert IU to mg per package insert).

pediatrico.notes: Contraindicated in patients with hypersensitivity to polymyxins.

neonatal.dose: Dose per NICU protocol; reserve use.

stability:
## Stability

- Use after dilution per package insert.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Irritability, ataxia, perioral paresthesia, nephrotoxicity.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — Polymyxin B Sulfate (code 0486, ATC J01XB).
